integrity.description: The agent used the Blender/Bonsai GUI to add organizations and assign actors.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with Blender panels (Project Directory, Object Properties, etc.) rather than terminal windows running Python scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent actually assigned the actor to the elements via the UI rather than just renaming objects.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender/Bonsai Actor assignment UI is visible with the correct organization selected, rather than just text typed into a generic text editor or object name field.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file using the Blender/Bonsai save/export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Export IFC' or 'Save As' dialog in Blender is used, or the top window bar reflects the new '.ifc' filename, confirming genuine software interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The FZK-Haus model contains exactly 11 'IfcWindow' elements. The required organizations to be added are 'Karlsruhe Institute of Technology', 'ArchitekturBuro FZK', and 'KlarGlas GmbH'. The target actor to be assigned to all 11 windows is 'KlarGlas GmbH'. The final output file must be saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_stakeholders.ifc'.
